expectations.text: manifest.json exists with UUID in reverse-DNS format
expectations.evidence: manifest.json exists at com.dbroesch.obs.sdPlugin/manifest.json with UUID: "com.dbroesch.obs" — valid reverse-DNS format.

expectations.text: manifest.json contains all 4 action UUIDs (record, stream, mute, scene)
manifest.json has CodePath pointing to bin/plugin.js

expectations.evidence: src/plugin.ts exists and line 35 calls: await streamDeck.connect().
All 4 files found: src/actions/record.ts, src/actions/stream.ts, src/actions/mute.ts, src/actions/scene.ts.

expectations.text: Scene action uses setTitle() to show current scene name
expectations.evidence: src/actions/scene.ts line 106: await actionInst.setTitle(label) where label is derived from sceneName via this.abbreviate(sceneName), showing the current scene name on the button.
rollup.config.mjs exists at outputs/rollup.config.mjs, configuring input as src/plugin.ts and output as com.dbroesch.obs.sdPlugin/bin/plugin.js.

expectations.text: No PKCE OAuth code included (not needed for OBS WebSocket)
expectations.evidence: Grep for pkce, code_verifier, code_challenge, oauth, OAuth, PKCE across all output files returned no matches.

expectations.text: OBS WebSocket connection logic is present
expectations.evidence: src/obs/client.ts implements a full OBSClient class using obs-websocket-js, connecting via ws://${host}:${port} with password, and handling reconnection, events, and OBS requests (GetRecordStatus, ToggleRecord, GetStreamStatus, etc.).

suggestions: The scene action registers OBS event listeners inside onWillAppear without cleanup — multiple button appearances could stack duplicate listeners.
SceneAction extends SingletonAction but registers per-instance OBS event listeners, which may cause issues if multiple scene buttons are configured.
The rollup.config.mjs emits an empty reloader.js asset in watch mode which is a no-op — a real reloader would need actual content.
Consider adding a stream.ts / record.ts inspection to confirm those action files also have correct implementations.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score: 10/10 assertions passed. The plugin structure is complete and correct — manifest, all 4 actions, OBS WebSocket integration via obs-websocket-js, setTitle usage in the scene action, rollup config, and @elgato/streamdeck dependency are all present. No PKCE/OAuth code was included. Minor code quality concerns around event listener lifecycle management in the scene action, but none affect the graded assertions.
